negativenumbers.question: The temperatures in five cities are: Aberdeen −4°C, Belfast −1°C, Cardiff 3°C, Dover 2°C, Edinburgh −6°C. What is the difference between the warmest and coldest cities?
negativenumbers.explanation: Warmest: Cardiff at 3°C. Coldest: Edinburgh at −6°C. Difference: 3 − (−6) = 3 + 6 = 9°C. ✓

negativenumbers.question: Oliver has −£8 in his account. He receives £15, spends £22, then receives £10. What is his final balance?
negativenumbers.explanation: Start: −£8. Receive £15: −8 + 15 = £7. Spend £22: 7 − 22 = −£15. Receive £10: −15 + 10 = −£5. His final balance is −£5. ✓

negativenumbers.question: A sequence starts: 20, 14, 8, 2, ... What is the first negative term in this sequence?
negativenumbers.explanation: The rule is subtract 6 each time. Term 1: 20, Term 2: 14, Term 3: 8, Term 4: 2, Term 5: 2 − 6 = −4. The first negative term is −4. ✓

negativenumbers.question: The temperature at the top of a mountain is −14°C. At the bottom it is 10°C. A hiker is halfway up, where the temperature is exactly halfway between the top and bottom values. What is the temperature where the hiker is?
negativenumbers.explanation: Difference between bottom and top: 10 − (−14) = 24°C. Half of 24 = 12. Temperature halfway: 10 − 12 = −2°C. Or equivalently: −14 + 12 = −2°C. ✓

negativenumbers.question: In a science experiment, a liquid starts at 6°C. It is cooled by 4°C every minute. After how many minutes will the temperature first be below −15°C?
negativenumbers.explanation: Start: 6°C. After 1 min: 6 − 4 = 2°C. After 2 min: 2 − 4 = −2°C. After 3 min: −2 − 4 = −6°C. After 4 min: −6 − 4 = −10°C. After 5 min: −10 − 4 = −14°C (not below −15). After 6 min: −14 − 4 = −18°C (below −15). The answer is 6 minutes. ✓

primenumbers.question: Which of these numbers is a prime number?
primenumbers.explanation: A prime number only has two factors: 1 and itself. 17 can only be divided by 1 and 17, so it's prime. The others have more factors. ✓

primenumbers.question: A maths challenge card asks: how many factors does 24 have?
primenumbers.explanation: The factors of 24 are: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 24. Count them: that's 8 factors. ✓

primenumbers.question: Emma wants to arrange 18 chairs in equal rows with no chairs left over. Which of these is NOT a possible number of rows?
primenumbers.explanation: The number of rows must be a factor of 18. Factors of 18 are: 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 18. The number 4 is NOT a factor of 18. ✓

primenumbers.question: What is the smallest prime number?
primenumbers.explanation: The smallest prime number is 2. It's the only even prime number! (1 is not considered prime, and 0 is not prime either.) ✓

primenumbers.question: Which number is both a factor of 30 and a factor of 45?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 30: 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 10, 15, 30. Factors of 45: 1, 3, 5, 9, 15, 45. Common factors include 1, 3, 5, and 15. From the options, 15 is a factor of both. ✓

primenumbers.question: Jake has 36 chocolates. He wants to share them equally among his friends with none left over. Which number of friends is NOT possible?
primenumbers.explanation: The number of friends must be a factor of 36. Factors of 36: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 9, 12, 18, 36. The number 5 is NOT a factor of 36. ✓

primenumbers.question: A homework sheet asks pupils to list every prime number between 20 and 30. How many prime numbers are there between 20 and 30?
primenumbers.explanation: Prime numbers between 20 and 30 are: 23 and 29. That's 2 prime numbers. ✓

primenumbers.question: A teacher has 12 red pencils and 18 blue pencils. She wants to put them into equal groups with the same number of each colour. What is the largest group size? (Find the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 12 and 18.)
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 12: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 12. Factors of 18: 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 18. Common factors: 1, 2, 3, 6. The highest is 6. ✓

primenumbers.question: Which of these is NOT a prime number?
primenumbers.explanation: 27 can be divided by 1, 3, 9, and 27, so it has more than two factors. Therefore 27 is NOT prime. All the others are prime. ✓

primenumbers.question: Sophie is making gift bags. She has 20 sweets and 30 stickers. She wants each bag to have the same number of sweets and the same number of stickers, with none left over. What is the maximum number of gift bags she can make?
primenumbers.explanation: Find the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 20 and 30. Factors of 20: 1, 2, 4, 5, 10, 20. Factors of 30: 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 10, 15, 30. The HCF is 10, so she can make 10 gift bags. ✓

primenumbers.question: Two flashing lights start together. One flashes every 4 seconds and the other every 6 seconds. After how many seconds will they next flash at the same time? (Find the smallest number that is a multiple of both 4 and 6.)
primenumbers.explanation: Multiples of 4: 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24... Multiples of 6: 6, 12, 18, 24, 30... The smallest common multiple is 12. ✓

primenumbers.question: In class, the teacher writes 17 on the board and asks how many numbers divide into it exactly. How many factors does the number 17 have?
primenumbers.explanation: 17 is a prime number, so it only has two factors: 1 and 17. ✓

primenumbers.question: Tom says 'All odd numbers are prime'. Is he correct?
primenumbers.explanation: Tom is wrong. A prime number (a number that can only be divided by 1 and itself) doesn't have to be odd. 9 is odd but NOT prime because 3 × 3 = 9, so it has an extra factor (a number that divides into it evenly). Other odd numbers that aren't prime: 15, 21, 25, 27. ✓

primenumbers.question: A teacher has 24 pencils and 36 erasers. She wants to make identical packs with the same number of pencils and erasers in each pack, with nothing left over. What is the largest number of packs she can make?
primenumbers.explanation: Find the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 24 and 36. Factors of 24: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 24. Factors of 36: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 9, 12, 18, 36. The HCF is 12, so she can make 12 packs. ✓

primenumbers.question: In a quiz, the question says: which number below has exactly 3 factors?
primenumbers.explanation: A number with exactly 3 factors must be the square of a prime. 9 = 3² and has factors: 1, 3, 9. That's exactly 3 factors. ✓

primenumbers.question: Lucy is arranging 42 books on shelves. Each shelf must have the same number of books. Which of these is a possible number of books per shelf?
primenumbers.explanation: The number must be a factor of 42. Factors of 42: 1, 2, 3, 6, 7, 14, 21, 42. From the options, 14 is a factor of 42. ✓

primenumbers.question: A florist has 15 roses and 25 tulips. She wants to make identical bunches using all the flowers, with the same number of each type in every bunch. What is the biggest number of bunches she can make? (Find the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 15 and 25.)
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 15: 1, 3, 5, 15. Factors of 25: 1, 5, 25. Common factors: 1, 5. The highest is 5. ✓

primenumbers.question: Ben's phone buzzes every 6 minutes and chimes every 8 minutes. If they both happen together now, how many minutes until they both happen together again?
primenumbers.explanation: Find the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 6 and 8. Multiples of 6: 6, 12, 18, 24, 30... Multiples of 8: 8, 16, 24, 32... The LCM is 24 minutes. ✓

primenumbers.question: In a classroom competition, pupils score a point for each factor a number has. Which of these numbers would score the most points?
primenumbers.explanation: Count factors: 20 has 6 factors (1,2,4,5,10,20); 24 has 8 factors (1,2,3,4,6,8,12,24); 28 has 6 factors; 26 has 4 factors (1,2,13,26); 32 has 6 factors. 24 has the most factors with 8. ✓

primenumbers.question: Mia and Oliver are running laps. Mia completes a lap every 4 minutes and Oliver every 5 minutes. If they start together, after how many minutes will they both be at the starting point together again?
primenumbers.explanation: Find the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 4 and 5. Multiples of 4: 4, 8, 12, 16, 20... Multiples of 5: 5, 10, 15, 20... The LCM is 20 minutes. ✓

primenumbers.question: Olivia is sorting number cards into 'prime' and 'not prime' piles. Which of these numbers should go in the prime pile?
primenumbers.explanation: 29 can only be divided by 1 and 29, so it is prime. All others are composite. ✓

primenumbers.question: Jack is listing all the numbers that divide exactly into 30. How many factors does 30 have?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 30 are: 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 10, 15, and 30. That's 8 factors. ✓

primenumbers.question: On a factor tree challenge card, you must break 72 down into prime factors. What is the prime factorisation of 72?

A PE teacher has 30 footballs and 45 tennis balls. She wants to share them equally into boxes with the same number of each type, using all the balls. What is the largest number of boxes she can fill? (Find the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 30 and 45.)
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 30: 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 10, 15, 30. Factors of 45: 1, 3, 5, 9, 15, 45. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 15. ✓

primenumbers.question: Two buses leave the station at the same time. Bus A comes every 16 minutes and Bus B every 24 minutes. How many minutes until they both leave together again? (Find the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 16 and 24.)
primenumbers.explanation: Multiples of 16: 16, 32, 48... Multiples of 24: 24, 48, 72... The Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) is 48. ✓

primenumbers.question: What is the largest prime number less than 30?
primenumbers.explanation: Testing: 28 = 2 × 14, 27 = 3 × 9, 25 = 5 × 5 (all composite). 29 is only divisible by 1 and 29, so it's prime. ✓

primenumbers.question: A PE teacher wants to split 28 pupils into two equal teams in every possible way. How many different factor pairs does 28 have?
primenumbers.explanation: Factor pairs of 28 are: (1, 28), (2, 14), and (4, 7). That's 3 different pairs. ✓

primenumbers.question: Oliver is finding the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 40 and 60. What answer should he get?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 40: 1, 2, 4, 5, 8, 10, 20, 40. Factors of 60: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 10, 12, 15, 20, 30, 60. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 20. ✓

primenumbers.question: On a factor tree worksheet, you break 42 into its prime factors, then add them up. What is the sum of the prime factors of 42?
primenumbers.explanation: 42 = 2 × 3 × 7. The prime factors are 2, 3, and 7. Sum: 2 + 3 + 7 = 12. ✓

primenumbers.question: What is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 14 and 21?
primenumbers.explanation: Multiples of 14: 14, 28, 42, 56... Multiples of 21: 21, 42, 63... The Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) is 42. ✓

primenumbers.question: Which of these is a prime number?
primenumbers.explanation: Check each: 51 = 3 × 17, 52 = 4 × 13, 55 = 5 × 11, 57 = 3 × 19 — these are all composite. Only 59 is prime (divisible only by 1 and 59). ✓

primenumbers.question: For homework, Emily must find all the factors of 50. How many factors does 50 have?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 50 are: 1, 2, 5, 10, 25, and 50. That's 6 factors. ✓

primenumbers.question: In a maths challenge, pupils must break 90 down into its prime factors. What is the prime factorisation of 90?

A baker has 28 cupcakes and 42 biscuits to pack into identical party bags, sharing them out equally with none left over. What is the greatest number of bags he can make? (Find the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 28 and 42.)
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 28: 1, 2, 4, 7, 14, 28. Factors of 42: 1, 2, 3, 6, 7, 14, 21, 42. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 14. ✓

primenumbers.question: Hot dogs come in packs of 10 and rolls come in packs of 15. What is the smallest number of each you need to buy so you have the same number of hot dogs and rolls? (Find the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 10 and 15.)
primenumbers.explanation: Multiples of 10: 10, 20, 30, 40... Multiples of 15: 15, 30, 45... The Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) is 30. ✓

primenumbers.question: Amy knows the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of two numbers is 8 and their Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) is 96. What is the product of the two numbers?
primenumbers.explanation: For any two numbers: Highest Common Factor (HCF) × Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) = Product. So 8 × 96 = 768. ✓

primenumbers.question: Which of these is a prime number between 40 and 50?
primenumbers.explanation: Testing: 42 = 2 × 21, 45 = 5 × 9, 46 = 2 × 23, 48 = 6 × 8 (all composite). 43 is only divisible by 1 and 43, so it's prime. ✓

primenumbers.question: Noah needs to find the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of three numbers: 15, 20, and 25. What is the HCF?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 15: 1, 3, 5, 15. Factors of 20: 1, 2, 4, 5, 10, 20. Factors of 25: 1, 5, 25. The highest common factor is 5. ✓

primenumbers.question: A chocolatier is designing boxes so that 40 chocolates sit in a perfect rectangle. How many different factor pairs does 40 have?
primenumbers.explanation: Factor pairs of 40: (1, 40), (2, 20), (4, 10), (5, 8). That's 4 different pairs. ✓

primenumbers.question: For a maths quiz bonus round, you must add up every prime number between 10 and 20. What is the sum of all prime numbers between 10 and 20?
primenumbers.explanation: Prime numbers between 10 and 20: 11, 13, 17, 19. Sum: 11 + 13 + 17 + 19 = 60. ✓

primenumbers.question: Bus A leaves the station every 8 minutes and Bus B leaves every 14 minutes. They both leave at 9:00 am. What is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 8 and 14 to find when they next leave together?
primenumbers.explanation: Multiples of 8: 8, 16, 24, 32, 40, 48, 56... Multiples of 14: 14, 28, 42, 56... The Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) is 56. ✓

primenumbers.question: Sam says all of these numbers are prime, but one of them is not. Which of these numbers is NOT prime?
primenumbers.explanation: 39 is not a prime number because it can be divided by 3 and 13 (3 × 13 = 39). A prime number can only be divided by 1 and itself. All the other numbers can only be divided by 1 and themselves, so they are prime. ✓

primenumbers.question: Mrs Jones asks her class to find all the ways 48 chairs can be arranged in equal rows. How many different factors does 48 have?
For her homework, Amira must write 100 as a product of prime numbers. What is the prime factorisation of 100?
A florist has 54 roses and 72 tulips. She wants to make identical bouquets using all the flowers. What is the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 54 and 72?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 54: 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 18, 27, 54. Factors of 72: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 9, 12, 18, 24, 36, 72. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 18. ✓

primenumbers.question: Two lighthouses flash their lights. One flashes every 9 seconds and the other every 12 seconds. What is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 9 and 12 to find when they next flash together?
primenumbers.explanation: Multiples of 9: 9, 18, 27, 36... Multiples of 12: 12, 24, 36... The Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) is 36. ✓

primenumbers.question: Lily finds the prime factors of 56. What is the product of all the different prime factors?
primenumbers.explanation: 56 = 2³ × 7. The different prime factors are 2 and 7. Product: 2 × 7 = 14. ✓

primenumbers.question: In a number hunt, Zara must find the first prime number after 60 on the number line. Which is the smallest prime number greater than 60?
primenumbers.explanation: 61 is only divisible by 1 and 61, so it's prime. It's the smallest prime > 60. ✓

primenumbers.question: Harry is tiling a wall with 32 square tiles arranged in a rectangle. How many different factor pairs does 32 have?
primenumbers.explanation: Factor pairs of 32: (1, 32), (2, 16), (4, 8). That's 3 different pairs. ✓

primenumbers.question: A craft teacher has 35 red beads and 49 blue beads to divide equally into bags with the same mix. What is the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 35 and 49?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 35: 1, 5, 7, 35. Factors of 49: 1, 7, 49. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 7. ✓

primenumbers.question: Class 5 has PE every 6 school days and art every 9 school days. Both happen today. What is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 6 and 9 to find when they next fall on the same day?
primenumbers.explanation: Multiples of 6: 6, 12, 18, 24... Multiples of 9: 9, 18, 27... The Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) is 18. ✓

primenumbers.question: Which of the following is a prime number?
primenumbers.explanation: 63 = 9 × 7, 65 = 5 × 13, 68 = 4 × 17, 69 = 3 × 23 — all composite. Only 71 is prime (divisible only by 1 and 71). ✓

primenumbers.question: A teacher arranges 36 chairs into equal rows. She lists every possible row size. How many factors does 36 have?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 36: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 9, 12, 18, 36. That's 9 factors. ✓

primenumbers.question: What is the prime factorisation of 84?
A librarian has 32 fiction books and 48 non-fiction books to display in equal stacks with the same number of each type. What is the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 32 and 48?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 32: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32. Factors of 48: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 24, 48. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 16. ✓

primenumbers.question: Two runners circle a track. One completes a lap every 20 seconds, the other every 30 seconds. What is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 20 and 30 to find when they next cross the start line together?
primenumbers.explanation: Multiples of 20: 20, 40, 60... Multiples of 30: 30, 60, 90... The Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) is 60. ✓

primenumbers.question: Ethan needs to find the smallest number divisible by both 7 and 9. What is this number?
primenumbers.explanation: This is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 7 and 9. Since 7 and 9 share no common factors, LCM = 7 × 9 = 63. ✓

primenumbers.question: One of these numbers is composite. Which one?
primenumbers.explanation: 77 = 7 × 11, so it is composite. All others are prime. ✓

primenumbers.question: How many different factor pairs does 45 have?
primenumbers.explanation: Factor pairs of 45: (1, 45), (3, 15), (5, 9). That's 3 different pairs. ✓

primenumbers.question: On a puzzle card, Charlotte must find the prime factors of 66 and then add them together. What is the sum of the prime factors of 66?
primenumbers.explanation: Find the prime factors of 66: 66 = 2 × 33 = 2 × 3 × 11. The prime factors are 2, 3, and 11. Their sum is 2 + 3 + 11 = 16. ✓

primenumbers.question: What is the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 56 and 84?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 56: 1, 2, 4, 7, 8, 14, 28, 56. Factors of 84: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 7, 12, 14, 21, 28, 42, 84. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 28. ✓

primenumbers.question: At sports day, the drum beats every 4 seconds and the whistle blows every 10 seconds. They start together. What is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 4 and 10 to find when they next happen at the same time?
primenumbers.explanation: Multiples of 4: 4, 8, 12, 16, 20... Multiples of 10: 10, 20, 30... The Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) is 20. ✓

primenumbers.question: A quiz master asks: what is the biggest prime number you can find that is still less than 70? Which is the largest prime number less than 70?
primenumbers.explanation: Testing: 69 = 3 × 23, 68 = 4 × 17 (composite). 67 is only divisible by 1 and 67, so it's prime. ✓

primenumbers.question: A school hall has 60 seats to arrange in equal rows for the assembly. How many factors does 60 have?
A quiz card asks you to express 120 using only prime factors. What is the prime factorisation of 120?
A gardener has 18 daffodils and 27 crocuses to plant in identical groups using every bulb. What is the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 18 and 27?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 18: 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 18. Factors of 27: 1, 3, 9, 27. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 9. ✓

primenumbers.question: A clock chimes every 5 minutes and a cuckoo clock calls every 8 minutes. They both sound at noon. What is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 5 and 8?
primenumbers.explanation: Since 5 and 8 have no common factors, the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) = 5 × 8 = 40. ✓

primenumbers.question: Only one of these numbers is prime. Which one?
Sophie knows the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of two numbers is 12 and their Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) is 120. What is the product?
primenumbers.explanation: There is a useful rule: for any two numbers, HCF × LCM = the product of the two numbers. Here, Highest Common Factor (HCF) = 12 and Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) = 120. So the product = 12 × 120 = 1,440. ✓

primenumbers.question: How many different factor pairs does 50 have?
primenumbers.explanation: Factor pairs of 50: (1, 50), (2, 25), (5, 10). That's 3 pairs. ✓

primenumbers.question: What is the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 75 and 100?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 75: 1, 3, 5, 15, 25, 75. Factors of 100: 1, 2, 4, 5, 10, 20, 25, 50, 100. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 25. ✓

primenumbers.question: What is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 11 and 13?
primenumbers.explanation: Since 11 and 13 are both prime with no common factors, the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) = 11 × 13 = 143. ✓

primenumbers.question: Which is the smallest prime number greater than 80?
primenumbers.explanation: Testing: 81 = 9², 82 = 2 × 41 (composite). 83 is only divisible by 1 and 83, so it's prime. ✓

primenumbers.question: What is the sum of the prime factors of 105?
Tom has 64 football stickers to stick evenly across pages of his album. How many factors does 64 have?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 64: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64. That's 7 factors. ✓

primenumbers.question: Mr Patel asks his class to draw a factor tree for 45. What is the prime factorisation of 45?
What is the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 63 and 81?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 63: 1, 3, 7, 9, 21, 63. Factors of 81: 1, 3, 9, 27, 81. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 9. ✓

primenumbers.question: What is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 25 and 30?
primenumbers.explanation: Multiples of 25: 25, 50, 75, 100, 125, 150... Multiples of 30: 30, 60, 90, 120, 150... The Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) is 150. ✓

primenumbers.question: Only one of these is NOT a prime number. Which one?
primenumbers.explanation: 99 = 9 × 11, so it is composite. All others are prime. ✓

primenumbers.question: How many different factor pairs does 60 have?
Year 5 collected 44 cans and 66 bottles for recycling. They want to sort them into equal groups. What is the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 44 and 66?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 44: 1, 2, 4, 11, 22, 44. Factors of 66: 1, 2, 3, 6, 11, 22, 33, 66. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 22. ✓

primenumbers.question: A school bell rings every 7 minutes for one class and every 10 minutes for another. What is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 7 and 10 to find when they next ring at the same time?
primenumbers.explanation: Since 7 and 10 have no common factors, the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) = 7 × 10 = 70. ✓

primenumbers.question: Which is the largest prime number less than 100?
primenumbers.explanation: Testing: 99 = 9 × 11, 98 = 2 × 49 (composite). 97 is only divisible by 1 and 97, so it's prime. ✓

primenumbers.question: A baker made 72 cupcakes and wants to know every way to pack them equally into boxes. How many factors does 72 have?
During a maths relay, your team must find the prime factorisation of 126. What is the prime factorisation of 126?
What is the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of three numbers: 24, 36, and 48?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 24: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 24. Factors of 36: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 9, 12, 18, 36. Factors of 48: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 24, 48. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 12. ✓

primenumbers.question: Lily waters her tomatoes every 4 days and her sunflowers every 6 days. She waters both today. What is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 4 and 6 to find when she next waters both on the same day?
primenumbers.explanation: Multiples of 4: 4, 8, 12, 16... Multiples of 6: 6, 12, 18... The Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) is 12. ✓

primenumbers.question: Which of these is a prime number between 50 and 60?
primenumbers.explanation: 51 = 3 × 17, 54 = 2 × 27, 55 = 5 × 11, 57 = 3 × 19 — all composite. Only 59 is prime (divisible only by 1 and 59). ✓

primenumbers.question: Mia knows the product of two numbers is 180 and their Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 6. What is their Lowest Common Multiple (LCM)?
primenumbers.explanation: Highest Common Factor (HCF) × Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) = Product. So 6 × LCM = 180. Therefore LCM = 180 ÷ 6 = 30. ✓

primenumbers.question: How many different factor pairs does 72 have?
What is the sum of all the prime numbers less than 15?
A sweet shop owner has 50 toffees and 80 chocolates to pack into identical party bags. What is the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 50 and 80?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 50: 1, 2, 5, 10, 25, 50. Factors of 80: 1, 2, 4, 5, 8, 10, 16, 20, 40, 80. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 10. ✓

primenumbers.question: Train A departs every 18 minutes and Train B every 24 minutes from the same platform. What is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 18 and 24 to find when they next depart together?
primenumbers.explanation: Multiples of 18: 18, 36, 54, 72... Multiples of 24: 24, 48, 72... The Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) is 72. ✓

primenumbers.question: Which is the smallest prime number?
primenumbers.explanation: 2 is the smallest prime number. It's also the only even prime. ✓

primenumbers.question: Grandma knitted 81 squares for a patchwork blanket. She wants to sew them into equal rows. How many factors does 81 have?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 81: 1, 3, 9, 27, 81. That's 5 factors. ✓

primenumbers.question: What is the prime factorisation of 54?
What is the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 90 and 135?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 90: 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 9, 10, 15, 18, 30, 45, 90. Factors of 135: 1, 3, 5, 9, 15, 27, 45, 135. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 45. ✓

primenumbers.question: What is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 3 and 7?
primenumbers.explanation: Since 3 and 7 are both prime with no common factors, the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) = 3 × 7 = 21. ✓

primenumbers.question: Which number has more than two factors?
primenumbers.explanation: 117 = 9 × 13, so it has more than two factors and is composite. 109, 113, 127, and 131 are all prime (only divisible by 1 and themselves). ✓

primenumbers.question: How many different factor pairs does 100 have?
primenumbers.explanation: The factors of 100 are: 1, 2, 4, 5, 10, 20, 25, 50, 100. Pair them up: (1, 100), (2, 50), (4, 25), (5, 20), (10, 10). That gives 5 different factor pairs. ✓

primenumbers.question: What is the sum of the prime factors of 70?
primenumbers.explanation: Find the prime factors of 70: 70 = 2 × 35 = 2 × 5 × 7. The prime factors are 2, 5, and 7. Their sum is 2 + 5 + 7 = 14. ✓

primenumbers.question: What is the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 52 and 78?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 52: 1, 2, 4, 13, 26, 52. Factors of 78: 1, 2, 3, 6, 13, 26, 39, 78. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 26. ✓

primenumbers.question: What is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 15 and 25?
primenumbers.explanation: Multiples of 15: 15, 30, 45, 60, 75... Multiples of 25: 25, 50, 75... The Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) is 75. ✓

primenumbers.question: Which number below can only be divided by 1 and itself?
What is the product of all prime factors of 110?
primenumbers.explanation: 110 = 2 × 5 × 11. Product of prime factors: 2 × 5 × 11 = 110. ✓

primenumbers.question: A farmer is planting 100 bulbs in equal rows across a flower bed. How many factors does 100 have?
What is the prime factorisation of 80?
What is the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 64 and 96?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 64: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64. Factors of 96: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, 48, 96. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 32. ✓

primenumbers.question: What is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 22 and 33?
primenumbers.explanation: Multiples of 22: 22, 44, 66, 88... Multiples of 33: 33, 66, 99... The Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) is 66. ✓

primenumbers.question: Which is the smallest prime number greater than 100?
primenumbers.explanation: 101 is only divisible by 1 and 101, so it's prime. It's the smallest prime > 100. ✓

primenumbers.question: How many different factor pairs does 84 have?
What is the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of three numbers: 30, 45, and 60?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 30: 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 10, 15, 30. Factors of 45: 1, 3, 5, 9, 15, 45. Factors of 60: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 10, 12, 15, 20, 30, 60. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 15. ✓

primenumbers.question: What is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 12 and 16?
primenumbers.explanation: Multiples of 12: 12, 24, 36, 48... Multiples of 16: 16, 32, 48... The Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) is 48. ✓

primenumbers.question: Which of these is a prime number between 70 and 80?
primenumbers.explanation: 72 = 8 × 9, 74 = 2 × 37, 75 = 3 × 25, 77 = 7 × 11 — all composite. Only 79 is prime (divisible only by 1 and 79). ✓

primenumbers.question: Noah knows two numbers have a product of 432 and a Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 12. What is their Lowest Common Multiple (LCM)?
primenumbers.explanation: Highest Common Factor (HCF) × Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) = Product. So 12 × LCM = 432. Therefore LCM = 432 ÷ 12 = 36. ✓

primenumbers.question: The school kitchen has 96 cartons of milk to place equally on trays. How many factors does 96 have?
What is the prime factorisation of 150?
What is the Highest Common Factor (HCF) of 85 and 119?
primenumbers.explanation: Factors of 85: 1, 5, 17, 85. Factors of 119: 1, 7, 17, 119. The Highest Common Factor (HCF) is 17. ✓

primenumbers.question: What is the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 28 and 35?
primenumbers.explanation: Multiples of 28: 28, 56, 84, 112, 140... Multiples of 35: 35, 70, 105, 140... The Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) is 140. ✓

primenumbers.question: Which is the only even prime number?
primenumbers.explanation: 2 is the only even prime number. All other even numbers are divisible by 2, making them composite. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Emily's rectangular garden is 8 metres long and 5 metres wide. What is the perimeter of her garden?
areaperimeter.explanation: To find the perimeter of a rectangle, add all four sides together. The formula is: Perimeter = 2 × (length + width). So: 2 × (8 + 5) = 2 × 13 = 26 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular swimming pool is 12 metres long and 6 metres wide. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: To find the area of a rectangle, multiply length by width. Area = length × width = 12 × 6 = 72 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Jake is building a fence around his square vegetable patch. Each side is 7 metres long. How much fencing does he need?
areaperimeter.explanation: A square has 4 equal sides. To find the perimeter, multiply the side length by 4. Perimeter = 4 × 7 = 28 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Sophie's bedroom floor is 5 metres long and 4 metres wide. She wants to carpet the entire floor. What area of carpet does she need?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = length × width = 5 × 4 = 20 square metres of carpet needed. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has a length of 9 cm and a width of 4 cm. What is the difference between its perimeter and its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: First find the perimeter: 2 × (9 + 4) = 2 × 13 = 26 cm. Then find the area: 9 × 4 = 36 cm². The difference is 36 - 26 = 10. Note: we're comparing numbers, not units. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Tom's rectangular football pitch is 90 metres long and 60 metres wide. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (length + width) = 2 × (90 + 60) = 2 × 150 = 300 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Lucy has a square picture frame where each side measures 15 cm. What is the area of the picture inside?
areaperimeter.explanation: For a square, area = side × side = 15 × 15 = 225 square cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has a perimeter of 24 cm. Its length is 8 cm. What is its width?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (length + width), so 24 = 2 × (8 + width). Divide both sides by 2: 12 = 8 + width. Therefore width = 12 - 8 = 4 cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Oliver is tiling his bathroom floor which is 3 metres long and 2 metres wide. Each tile covers 1 square metre. How many tiles does he need?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area of bathroom = 3 × 2 = 6 square metres. Each tile covers 1 square metre, so he needs 6 tiles. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Emma's rectangular garden has an area of 48 square metres and a width of 6 metres. What is the length?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = length × width, so 48 = length × 6. To find length, divide: 48 ÷ 6 = 8 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square playground has a perimeter of 40 metres. What is the length of each side?
areaperimeter.explanation: A square has 4 equal sides. Perimeter = 4 × side length, so 40 = 4 × side. Divide: 40 ÷ 4 = 10 metres per side. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Ben's rectangular desk is 120 cm long and 80 cm wide. What is its area in square metres?
areaperimeter.explanation: First convert to metres: 120 cm = 1.2 m and 80 cm = 0.8 m. Area = 1.2 × 0.8 = 0.96 square metres. Or: area in cm² = 120 × 80 = 9600 cm², then divide by 10,000 to get m²: 9600 ÷ 10,000 = 0.96 m². ✓

areaperimeter.question: Sarah needs to put a border around her rectangular poster that is 40 cm long and 30 cm wide. What length of border does she need?
areaperimeter.explanation: The border goes around the perimeter. Perimeter = 2 × (40 + 30) = 2 × 70 = 140 cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square garden has an area of 64 square metres. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: If area = 64 m², and area of a square = side × side, then side × side = 64. So side = 8 m (because 8 × 8 = 64). Perimeter = 4 × 8 = 32 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Max's rectangular bedroom is twice as long as it is wide. If the width is 4 metres, what is the area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Width = 4 metres, and length is twice the width, so length = 2 × 4 = 8 metres. Area = 8 × 4 = 32 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: An L-shaped room is made of two rectangles. The first rectangle is 6m by 4m. The second rectangle is 4m by 3m. What is the total area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Find the area of each rectangle separately, then add them. First rectangle: 6 × 4 = 24 m². Second rectangle: 4 × 3 = 12 m². Total area: 24 + 12 = 36 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Charlie is painting a rectangular wall that is 5 metres high and 8 metres wide. Paint covers 10 square metres per tin. How many tins does he need?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area of wall = 5 × 8 = 40 square metres. Each tin covers 10 m², so number of tins = 40 ÷ 10 = 4 tins. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has a length of 12 cm and a perimeter of 32 cm. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (length + width), so 32 = 2 × (12 + width). Therefore 16 = 12 + width, so width = 4 cm. Area = 12 × 4 = 48 square cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Mia's rectangular garden path is 15 metres long and 2 metres wide. She wants to put stones along both long sides and both short sides. What total length of stones does she need?
areaperimeter.explanation: This is asking for the perimeter. Perimeter = 2 × (15 + 2) = 2 × 17 = 34 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square field has a perimeter of 200 metres. A farmer wants to plant grass on the entire field. What area needs to be planted?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 4 × side, so 200 = 4 × side. Side = 200 ÷ 4 = 50 metres. Area = 50 × 50 = 2500 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has width w cm and length (w + 5) cm. If w = 7, what is the perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: If w = 7, then width = 7 cm and length = 7 + 5 = 12 cm. Perimeter = 2 × (7 + 12) = 2 × 19 = 38 cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Hannah has two rectangular flower beds. The first is 5m by 3m, and the second is 4m by 4m. Which has the greater area and by how much?
areaperimeter.explanation: First bed area: 5 × 3 = 15 m². Second bed area: 4 × 4 = 16 m². The second bed has greater area by 16 - 15 = 1 square metre. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular field is 30 metres long and 20 metres wide. A path 2 metres wide runs all the way around the inside edge. What is the area of the path?
areaperimeter.explanation: Total field area: 30 × 20 = 600 m². The path is 2m wide all around, so the inner rectangle (grass area) is (30 - 4) by (20 - 4) = 26 by 16. Inner area: 26 × 16 = 416 m². Path area = 600 - 416 = 184 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square has a perimeter of P cm. What is its area in terms of P?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter of square = 4 × side, so side = P ÷ 4. Area = side × side = (P ÷ 4) × (P ÷ 4) = P² ÷ 16. ✓

areaperimeter.question: James has a rectangular lawn that is 20m by 15m. He wants to create a flower border 1 metre wide all around the inside. What is the area of the flower border?
areaperimeter.explanation: Total lawn area: 20 × 15 = 300 m². After removing 1m border all around, inner lawn is (20 - 2) by (15 - 2) = 18 by 13. Inner area: 18 × 13 = 234 m². Border area = 300 - 234 = 66 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has dimensions 2x cm by 3x cm. If x = 4, what is the area?
areaperimeter.explanation: If x = 4, then dimensions are 2 × 4 = 8 cm and 3 × 4 = 12 cm. Area = 8 × 12 = 96 square cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Which of these rectangles has a different area from the others?
areaperimeter.explanation: Calculate each area: 9 × 4 = 36 cm², 12 × 3 = 36 cm², 18 × 2 = 36 cm², 8 × 5 = 40 cm², 6 × 6 = 36 cm². The rectangle that is 8 cm by 5 cm has a different area (40 cm²) from the others (36 cm²). ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular swimming pool has a length of (2y + 3) metres and a width of y metres. If y = 5, what is the perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: If y = 5, then length = (2 × 5) + 3 = 13 metres and width = 5 metres. Perimeter = 2 × (13 + 5) = 2 × 18 = 36 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A compound shape is made from a rectangle 10cm by 6cm with a square of side 3cm cut from one corner. What is the remaining area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Rectangle area: 10 × 6 = 60 cm². Square cut out: 3 × 3 = 9 cm². Remaining area: 60 - 9 = 51 square cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has a perimeter of 48 cm. Its length is three times its width. What is the area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Let width = w, then length = 3w. Perimeter = 2 × (w + 3w) = 2 × 4w = 8w. So 48 = 8w, which means w = 6 cm. Length = 3 × 6 = 18 cm. Area = 6 × 18 = 108 square cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular classroom is 10 metres long and 7 metres wide. What is the perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (length + width) = 2 × (10 + 7) = 2 × 17 = 34 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square park has sides of 9 metres. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area of a square = side × side = 9 × 9 = 81 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Noah's rectangular driveway is 15 metres long and 8 metres wide. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = length × width = 15 × 8 = 120 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square field has a perimeter of 60 metres. What is the length of each side?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 4 × side, so 60 = 4 × side. Side = 60 ÷ 4 = 15 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular rug has an area of 56 square metres and a width of 7 metres. What is its length?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = length × width, so 56 = length × 7. Length = 56 ÷ 7 = 8 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square patio has an area of 100 square metres. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: If area = 100, then side × side = 100, so side = 10m. Perimeter = 4 × 10 = 40 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Lily's rectangular book is 18 cm long and 6 cm wide. What is its perimeter?
A rectangle has a perimeter of 50 cm and a length of 15 cm. What is its width?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (length + width), so 50 = 2 × (15 + width). Therefore 25 = 15 + width, so width = 10 cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular tennis court is 20 metres long and 12 metres wide. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = length × width = 20 × 12 = 240 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square tile has sides of 11 cm. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 4 × side = 4 × 11 = 44 cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Oliver is fencing a rectangular garden that is 14 metres long and 9 metres wide. How much fencing does he need?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (14 + 9) = 2 × 23 = 46 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square room has a perimeter of 32 metres. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 4 × side, so side = 32 ÷ 4 = 8m. Area = 8 × 8 = 64 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has a length of 13 cm and a width of 5 cm. What is the difference between its perimeter and its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (13 + 5) = 36 cm. Area = 13 × 5 = 65 cm². Difference = 65 - 36 = 29. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Ava's rectangular poster is 25 cm long and 16 cm wide. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = 25 × 16 = 400 square cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square garden has sides of 12 metres. A path 1 metre wide runs around the outside. What is the perimeter of the outer edge of the path?
areaperimeter.explanation: The path adds 1m on each side, so outer square has sides of 12 + 1 + 1 = 14m. Perimeter = 4 × 14 = 56 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle is twice as long as it is wide. If the width is 6 cm, what is the perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Width = 6 cm, length = 2 × 6 = 12 cm. Perimeter = 2 × (12 + 6) = 2 × 18 = 36 cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Mason's rectangular yard is 22 metres long and 18 metres wide. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (22 + 18) = 2 × 40 = 80 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square has a perimeter of 84 cm. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Side = 84 ÷ 4 = 21 cm. Area = 21 × 21 = 441 square cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular window is 80 cm wide and 120 cm tall. What is its area in square metres?
areaperimeter.explanation: Convert to metres: 80 cm = 0.8 m, 120 cm = 1.2 m. Area = 0.8 × 1.2 = 0.96 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Harper is tiling a rectangular floor that is 7 metres long and 5 metres wide. Each tile covers 1 square metre. How many tiles are needed?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = 7 × 5 = 35 square metres. Each tile is 1 m², so 35 tiles needed. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square courtyard has an area of 144 square metres. What is the length of each side?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = side × side = 144, so side = 12 metres (because 12 × 12 = 144). ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has a perimeter of 44 cm and a width of 8 cm. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (L + W), so 44 = 2 × (L + 8). Therefore 22 = L + 8, L = 14 cm. Area = 14 × 8 = 112 cm². ✓

areaperimeter.question: Ethan's rectangular swimming pool is 16 metres long and 10 metres wide. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (16 + 10) = 2 × 26 = 52 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle is three times as long as it is wide. If the width is 5 metres, what is the area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Width = 5 m, length = 3 × 5 = 15 m. Area = 15 × 5 = 75 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square playground has sides of 18 metres. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = 18 × 18 = 324 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Zoe is putting a border around a rectangular picture that is 35 cm long and 25 cm wide. What length of border does she need?
areaperimeter.explanation: Border goes around perimeter. Perimeter = 2 × (35 + 25) = 2 × 60 = 120 cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular hall is 24 metres long and 15 metres wide. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = 24 × 15 = 360 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square carpet has a perimeter of 36 metres. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Side = 36 ÷ 4 = 9 metres. Area = 9 × 9 = 81 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has an area of 72 square cm and a length of 9 cm. What is its width?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = L × W, so 72 = 9 × W. Width = 72 ÷ 9 = 8 cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Leo's rectangular garden is 19 metres long and 11 metres wide. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (19 + 11) = 2 × 30 = 60 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: An L-shaped room is made of two rectangles. The first is 8m by 5m. The second is 6m by 4m. What is the total area?
areaperimeter.explanation: First rectangle: 8 × 5 = 40 m². Second rectangle: 6 × 4 = 24 m². Total: 40 + 24 = 64 m². ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square has an area of 169 square cm. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Side × side = 169, so side = 13 cm. Perimeter = 4 × 13 = 52 cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular car park is 45 metres long and 32 metres wide. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = 45 × 32 = 1,440 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has a length of 11 cm and a perimeter of 36 cm. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (11 + W), so 36 = 2 × (11 + W). Therefore 18 = 11 + W, W = 7 cm. Area = 11 × 7 = 77 cm². ✓

areaperimeter.question: Ruby is painting a square wall where each side is 4 metres. Paint covers 5 square metres per tin. How many tins does she need?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = 4 × 4 = 16 m². Each tin covers 5 m², so 16 ÷ 5 = 3.2. She needs 4 tins (must round up). ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has dimensions 4x cm by 5x cm. If x = 3, what is the area?
areaperimeter.explanation: If x = 3, dimensions are 4 × 3 = 12 cm and 5 × 3 = 15 cm. Area = 12 × 15 = 180 cm². ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular field is 50 metres long and 30 metres wide. A farmer wants to fence all four sides. How much fencing is needed?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (50 + 30) = 2 × 80 = 160 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square has a perimeter of P metres. What is its area in terms of P?
areaperimeter.explanation: Side = P ÷ 4. Area = side × side = (P ÷ 4) × (P ÷ 4) = P² ÷ 16. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Isabella's rectangular room is 13 metres long and 9 metres wide. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = 13 × 9 = 117 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square garden has an area of 225 square metres. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Side × side = 225, so side = 15 m. Perimeter = 4 × 15 = 60 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has a perimeter of 70 cm and a width of 12 cm. What is its length?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (L + 12), so 70 = 2 × (L + 12). Therefore 35 = L + 12, L = 23 cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Jack's rectangular vegetable patch is 17 metres long and 14 metres wide. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (17 + 14) = 2 × 31 = 62 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle is four times as long as it is wide. If the width is 6 metres, what is the perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Width = 6 m, length = 4 × 6 = 24 m. Perimeter = 2 × (24 + 6) = 2 × 30 = 60 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Four of these rectangles have the same area. Which one is different?
areaperimeter.explanation: Calculate each: 15×4=60, 20×3=60, 12×5=60, 10×7=70, 30×2=60. The 10×7 rectangle has different area. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A compound shape is made from a rectangle 12cm by 8cm with a square of side 4cm cut from one corner. What is the remaining area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Rectangle: 12 × 8 = 96 cm². Square cut out: 4 × 4 = 16 cm². Remaining: 96 - 16 = 80 cm². ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular lawn is 28 metres long and 18 metres wide. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = 28 × 18 = 504 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square has sides of 14 cm. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 4 × 14 = 56 cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has an area of 90 square metres and a width of 9 metres. What is its length?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = L × W, so 90 = L × 9. Length = 90 ÷ 9 = 10 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular office is 26 metres long and 19 metres wide. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (26 + 19) = 2 × 45 = 90 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square patio has a perimeter of 52 metres. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Side = 52 ÷ 4 = 13 m. Area = 13 × 13 = 169 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Mia is tiling a rectangular bathroom that is 4 metres long and 3 metres wide. Each tile is 0.5 metres by 0.5 metres. How many tiles does she need?
areaperimeter.explanation: Bathroom area = 4 × 3 = 12 m². Each tile = 0.5 × 0.5 = 0.25 m². Tiles needed = 12 ÷ 0.25 = 48 tiles. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has a perimeter of 56 cm and a length of 18 cm. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (18 + W), so 56 = 2 × (18 + W). Therefore 28 = 18 + W, W = 10 cm. Area = 18 × 10 = 180 cm². ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square room has sides of 16 metres. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = 16 × 16 = 256 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular park is 35 metres long and 28 metres wide. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (35 + 28) = 2 × 63 = 126 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has dimensions 3y metres by 7y metres. If y = 2, what is the area?
areaperimeter.explanation: If y = 2, dimensions are 3 × 2 = 6 m and 7 × 2 = 14 m. Area = 6 × 14 = 84 m². ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square has an area of 196 square cm. What is the length of each side?
areaperimeter.explanation: Side × side = 196, so side = 14 cm (because 14 × 14 = 196). ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular shed is 11 metres long and 8 metres wide. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = 11 × 8 = 88 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has a perimeter of 64 cm. Its length is 20 cm. What is its width?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (20 + W), so 64 = 2 × (20 + W). Therefore 32 = 20 + W, W = 12 cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Sophie's rectangular garden has an area of 84 square metres and a length of 12 metres. What is its width?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = L × W, so 84 = 12 × W. Width = 84 ÷ 12 = 7 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square fence encloses an area of 289 square metres. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Side × side = 289, so side = 17 m. Perimeter = 4 × 17 = 68 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular swimming pool has a length of (3z + 2) metres and a width of z metres. If z = 4, what is the area?
areaperimeter.explanation: If z = 4, length = (3 × 4) + 2 = 14 m and width = 4 m. Area = 14 × 4 = 56 m². ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has a perimeter of 60 cm. Its length is twice its width. What is the area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Let the width = w. The length = 2w. Perimeter = 2 × (2w + w) = 6w. So 6w = 60, which means w = 10 cm. Length = 2 × 10 = 20 cm. Area = 20 × 10 = 200 square cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Emma has two rectangular flower beds. The first is 9m by 6m, and the second is 8m by 7m. Which has the greater area and by how much?
areaperimeter.explanation: First bed: 9 × 6 = 54 m². Second bed: 8 × 7 = 56 m². Second bed is greater by 56 - 54 = 2 m². ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular field is 40 metres long and 25 metres wide. A path 3 metres wide runs around the inside edge. What is the area of the path?
areaperimeter.explanation: Field area: 40 × 25 = 1,000 m². The path is 3m wide on each side, so the inner rectangle is (40 − 6) × (25 − 6) = 34 × 19 = 646 m². Path area = 1,000 − 646 = 354 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square has a perimeter of 100 metres. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Side = 100 ÷ 4 = 25 m. Area = 25 × 25 = 625 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular meeting room is 21 metres long and 16 metres wide. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (21 + 16) = 2 × 37 = 74 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has a length of 14 cm and a width of 9 cm. What is the sum of its perimeter and area?
Oliver has a rectangular lawn that is 25m by 18m. He wants to create a flower border 2 metres wide all around the inside. What is the area of the flower border?
A square has sides of 13 metres. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = 13 × 13 = 169 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has an area of 105 square cm and a length of 15 cm. What is its perimeter?
A rectangular dance floor is 23 metres long and 17 metres wide. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = 23 × 17 = 391 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square has a perimeter of 68 cm. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Side = 68 ÷ 4 = 17 cm. Area = 17 × 17 = 289 square cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has a perimeter of 72 metres and a width of 14 metres. What is its length?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (L + 14), so 72 = 2 × (L + 14). Therefore 36 = L + 14, L = 22 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Lucy's rectangular courtyard is 27 metres long and 19 metres wide. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (27 + 19) = 2 × 46 = 92 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle is five times as long as it is wide. If the width is 4 metres, what is the area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Width = 4 m, length = 5 × 4 = 20 m. Area = 20 × 4 = 80 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square garden has an area of 361 square metres. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Side × side = 361, so side = 19 m. Perimeter = 4 × 19 = 76 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular poster is 42 cm long and 30 cm wide. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = 42 × 30 = 1,260 square cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has a perimeter of 90 cm and a length of 28 cm. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (length + width), so 90 = 2 × (28 + width). Divide both sides by 2: 45 = 28 + width. Width = 45 - 28 = 17 cm. Area = 28 × 17 = 476 square cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square has sides of 17 metres. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 4 × 17 = 68 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular stadium is 32 metres long and 24 metres wide. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = 32 × 24 = 768 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: An L-shaped room is made of two rectangles. The first is 10m by 7m. The second is 8m by 5m. What is the total area?
areaperimeter.explanation: First: 10 × 7 = 70 m². Second: 8 × 5 = 40 m². Total: 70 + 40 = 110 m². ✓

areaperimeter.question: A compound shape is made from a rectangle 15cm by 10cm with a square of side 5cm cut from one corner. What is the remaining area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Rectangle: 15 × 10 = 150 cm². Square: 5 × 5 = 25 cm². Remaining: 150 - 25 = 125 cm². ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square has a perimeter of 76 metres. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Side = 76 ÷ 4 = 19 m. Area = 19 × 19 = 361 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has an area of 126 square metres and a width of 9 metres. What is its length?
areaperimeter.explanation: Length = 126 ÷ 9 = 14 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Charlie's rectangular playground is 29 metres long and 21 metres wide. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (29 + 21) = 2 × 50 = 100 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has dimensions 6w metres by 8w metres. If w = 2, what is the perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: If w = 2, dimensions are 12m and 16m. Perimeter = 2 × (12 + 16) = 2 × 28 = 56 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square has sides of 20 metres. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = 20 × 20 = 400 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has a perimeter of 96 cm and a length of 30 cm. What is its area?
Amelia has a rectangular lawn that is 30m by 22m. She wants to create a flower border 1.5 metres wide all around the inside. What is the area of the flower border?
A rectangle has a perimeter of 100 metres. Its length is four times its width. What is the area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Let the width = w. The length = 4w. Perimeter = 2 × (w + 4w) = 10w. So 10w = 100, which means w = 10 m. Length = 4 × 10 = 40 m. Area = 40 × 10 = 400 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square has an area of 324 square cm. What is the length of each side?
areaperimeter.explanation: Side × side = 324, so side = 18 cm (because 18 × 18 = 324). ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangular warehouse is 38 metres long and 26 metres wide. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = 38 × 26 = 988 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has a perimeter of 84 cm and a width of 16 cm. What is its length?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (L + 16), so 84 = 2 × (L + 16). Therefore 42 = L + 16, L = 26 cm. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square courtyard has a perimeter of 88 metres. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Side = 88 ÷ 4 = 22 m. Area = 22 × 22 = 484 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Benjamin's rectangular study is 31 metres long and 23 metres wide. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Perimeter = 2 × (31 + 23) = 2 × 54 = 108 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle is six times as long as it is wide. If the width is 5 metres, what is the perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Width = 5 m, length = 6 × 5 = 30 m. Perimeter = 2 × (30 + 5) = 2 × 35 = 70 metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: Which rectangle does NOT have the same area as the others?
A rectangular field is 50 metres long and 36 metres wide. A path 2 metres wide runs around the inside edge. What is the area of the path?
areaperimeter.explanation: Field area: 50 × 36 = 1,800 m². The path is 2m wide on each side, so the inner rectangle is (50 − 4) × (36 − 4) = 46 × 32 = 1,472 m². Path area = 1,800 − 1,472 = 328 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A square has sides of 19 metres. What is its area?
areaperimeter.explanation: Area = 19 × 19 = 361 square metres. ✓

areaperimeter.question: A rectangle has an area of 144 square metres and a length of 16 metres. What is its perimeter?
areaperimeter.explanation: Width = 144 ÷ 16 = 9 m. Perimeter = 2 × (16 + 9) = 2 × 25 = 50 metres. ✓

volume.question: A rectangular box is 10 cm long, 5 cm wide, and 4 cm high. What is its volume?
volume.explanation: Volume of a cuboid = length × width × height. So: 10 × 5 × 4 = 200 cubic cm (or 200 cm³). ✓

volume.question: Emma has a cube-shaped storage box. Each edge is 6 cm long. What is the volume?
volume.explanation: Volume of a cube = edge × edge × edge. So: 6 × 6 × 6 = 216 cubic cm. ✓

volume.question: Tom's fish tank is 50 cm long, 30 cm wide, and 40 cm high. What is its volume in litres? (Remember: 1 litre = 1000 cm³)
volume.explanation: First find volume in cm³: 50 × 30 × 40 = 60,000 cm³. Then convert to litres: 60,000 ÷ 1000 = 60 litres. ✓

volume.question: A cuboid has a volume of 120 cm³, a length of 10 cm, and a width of 4 cm. What is its height?
volume.explanation: Volume = length × width × height, so 120 = 10 × 4 × height. This means 120 = 40 × height, so height = 120 ÷ 40 = 3 cm. ✓

volume.question: Sophie is packing books into a box that is 30 cm × 20 cm × 15 cm. What is the volume of the box?
A cube has a volume of 125 cm³. What is the length of each edge?
volume.explanation: Volume of cube = edge³, so edge³ = 125. We need to find what number multiplied by itself three times equals 125. 5 × 5 × 5 = 125, so each edge is 5 cm. ✓

volume.question: Jake's bedroom is 4 metres long, 3 metres wide, and 2.5 metres high. What is the volume of his bedroom in cubic metres?
volume.explanation: Volume = length × width × height = 4 × 3 × 2.5 = 30 cubic metres (or 30 m³). ✓

volume.question: A box measures 8 cm by 8 cm by 8 cm. Another box measures 16 cm by 4 cm by 8 cm. Which statement is true?
volume.explanation: First box: 8 × 8 × 8 = 512 cm³. Second box: 16 × 4 × 8 = 512 cm³. They have equal volumes. ✓

volume.question: Mia fills a container that is 20 cm long, 10 cm wide, and 5 cm high with water. How many millilitres of water does it hold? (1 cm³ = 1 ml)
volume.explanation: Volume = 20 × 10 × 5 = 1000 cm³. Since 1 cm³ = 1 ml, the container holds 1000 ml (or 1 litre). ✓

volume.question: A cuboid has dimensions 12 cm × 5 cm × 3 cm. What is its volume?
Oliver has two identical cube-shaped dice. Each die has edges of 2 cm. What is the total volume of both dice?
volume.explanation: Volume of one cube = 2 × 2 × 2 = 8 cm³. Two dice = 8 + 8 = 16 cm³. ✓

volume.question: A rectangular swimming pool is 25 metres long, 10 metres wide, and 2 metres deep. What is its volume?
volume.explanation: Volume = length × width × depth = 25 × 10 × 2 = 500 cubic metres (or 500 m³). ✓

volume.question: Lucy has a storage box with a volume of 2400 cm³. The base is 20 cm by 15 cm. How tall is the box?
volume.explanation: Volume = base area × height. Base area = 20 × 15 = 300 cm². So 2400 = 300 × height. Height = 2400 ÷ 300 = 8 cm. ✓

volume.question: A cuboid has dimensions x cm by 2x cm by 3 cm. If x = 5, what is the volume?
volume.explanation: If x = 5, the dimensions are: 5 cm × 10 cm × 3 cm (because 2x = 2 × 5 = 10). Volume = 5 × 10 × 3 = 150 cm³. ✓

volume.question: Ben's toy box is 60 cm long, 40 cm wide, and 50 cm tall. He fills it halfway with toys. What volume of space is occupied by the toys?
volume.explanation: Total volume = 60 × 40 × 50 = 120,000 cm³. Half filled means toys occupy 120,000 ÷ 2 = 60,000 cm³. ✓

volume.question: A cube and a cuboid both have the same volume of 64 cm³. The cube has edges of 4 cm. If the cuboid is 8 cm long and 2 cm wide, what is its height?
volume.explanation: The cuboid's volume is 64 cm³. Volume = length × width × height, so 64 = 8 × 2 × height. This gives 64 = 16 × height, so height = 64 ÷ 16 = 4 cm. ✓

volume.question: Hannah is filling a rectangular container that measures 50 cm by 30 cm by 20 cm. How many 1-litre bottles of water will she need to fill it completely? (1 litre = 1000 cm³)
volume.explanation: Volume of container = 50 × 30 × 20 = 30,000 cm³. Convert to litres: 30,000 ÷ 1000 = 30 litres. So she needs 30 bottles. ✓

volume.question: A cube has edges that are twice as long as another cube with 3 cm edges. What is the volume of the larger cube?
volume.explanation: The small cube has 3 cm edges. The larger cube has edges twice as long: 2 × 3 = 6 cm. Volume of larger cube = 6 × 6 × 6 = 216 cm³. ✓

volume.question: A rectangular box is 12 cm long, 6 cm wide, and 5 cm high. What is its volume?
volume.explanation: Volume = length × width × height = 12 × 6 × 5 = 360 cubic cm. ✓

volume.question: A cube has edges of 7 cm. What is its volume?
volume.explanation: Volume of cube = edge³ = 7 × 7 × 7 = 343 cubic cm. ✓

volume.question: Noah's storage container is 15 cm long, 8 cm wide, and 4 cm high. What is its volume?
A cube has a volume of 512 cubic cm. What is the length of each edge?
volume.explanation: edge³ = 512. Find what number × itself × itself = 512. 8 × 8 × 8 = 512, so edge = 8 cm. ✓

volume.question: A cuboid has a volume of 180 cm³, a length of 12 cm, and a width of 5 cm. What is its height?
volume.explanation: Volume = L × W × H, so 180 = 12 × 5 × H. Therefore 180 = 60 × H, H = 180 ÷ 60 = 3 cm. ✓

volume.explanation: If y = 4, dimensions are 8 × 20 × 6. Volume = 8 × 20 × 6 = 960 cm³. ✓

volume.explanation: If z = 5, dimensions are 3 × 5 = 15 cm, 4 × 5 = 20 cm, and 8 cm. Volume = 15 × 20 × 8 = 2,400 cubic cm. ✓

A cube has edges that are four times as long as another cube with 5 cm edges. What is the volume of the larger cube?
volume.explanation: Larger cube edge = 4 × 5 = 20 cm. Volume = 20 × 20 × 20 = 8,000 cm³. ✓

volume.question: A rectangular container is 60 cm long, 42 cm wide, and 28 cm high. What is its volume?

volume.explanation: One cube = 8 × 8 × 8 = 512 cm³. Eight cubes = 512 × 8 = 4,096 cm³. ✓

volume.question: A rectangular container measures 72 cm by 48 cm by 35 cm. What is its volume?

volume.explanation: If w = 5, dimensions are 2×5 = 10, 3×5 = 15, and 4×5 = 20. Volume = 10 × 15 × 20 = 3,000 cm³. ✓

volume.question: A box measures 76 cm by 54 cm by 38 cm. What is its volume?

A fish tank is 60 cm long, 30 cm wide, and 40 cm tall. Water fills it to three-quarters of its height. How many litres of water are in the tank? (1 litre = 1,000 cm\u00B3)
volume.explanation: Three-quarters of the height = 40 \u00D7 3/4 = 30 cm. Volume of water = 60 \u00D7 30 \u00D7 30 = 54,000 cm\u00B3. Convert to litres: 54,000 \u00F7 1,000 = 54 litres. \u2713

volume.question: Two cuboids have the same volume. The first cuboid measures 8 cm \u00D7 6 cm \u00D7 5 cm. The second cuboid is 10 cm long and 4 cm wide. What is the height of the second cuboid?
volume.explanation: Volume of first cuboid = 8 \u00D7 6 \u00D7 5 = 240 cm\u00B3. The second cuboid has the same volume, so 10 \u00D7 4 \u00D7 height = 240. That gives 40 \u00D7 height = 240, so height = 240 \u00F7 40 = 6 cm. \u2713

volume.question: A paddling pool is 20 m long, 10 m wide, and 1.5 m deep. It takes 5 hours to fill completely. How many litres of water flow in per minute? (1 m\u00B3 = 1,000 litres)
volume.explanation: Volume = 20 \u00D7 10 \u00D7 1.5 = 300 m\u00B3. Convert to litres: 300 \u00D7 1,000 = 300,000 litres. Time in minutes: 5 \u00D7 60 = 300 minutes. Flow rate = 300,000 \u00F7 300 = 1,000 litres per minute. \u2713

volume.question: A cube has edges of length (n + 2) cm. If the volume of the cube is 125 cm\u00B3, what is the value of n?
volume.explanation: Volume of a cube = edge\u00B3. So (n + 2)\u00B3 = 125. Since 5 \u00D7 5 \u00D7 5 = 125, we know n + 2 = 5. Therefore n = 5 \u2212 2 = 3. \u2713

volume.question: Which container holds more water: a rectangular box measuring 30 cm \u00D7 20 cm \u00D7 15 cm, or a cube with edges of 25 cm? How much more does the larger one hold, in cm\u00B3?
volume.explanation: Box volume = 30 \u00D7 20 \u00D7 15 = 9,000 cm\u00B3. Cube volume = 25 \u00D7 25 \u00D7 25 = 15,625 cm\u00B3. The cube holds more. Difference = 15,625 \u2212 9,000 = 6,625 cm\u00B3. \u2713

volume.question: A rectangular tank has a base measuring 40 cm by 25 cm and contains some water. When a stone is placed in the tank, the water level rises by 2 cm. What is the volume of the stone?
volume.explanation: The stone displaces water equal to its own volume. The displaced water forms a layer on top: base area \u00D7 rise in height = 40 \u00D7 25 \u00D7 2 = 2,000 cm\u00B3. So the volume of the stone is 2,000 cm\u00B3. \u2713

volume.question: A tap fills a rectangular tank at a rate of 4 litres per minute. The tank is 80 cm long, 50 cm wide, and 30 cm high. How many minutes does it take to fill the tank completely? (1 litre = 1,000 cm\u00B3)
volume.explanation: Volume of tank = 80 \u00D7 50 \u00D7 30 = 120,000 cm\u00B3. Convert to litres: 120,000 \u00F7 1,000 = 120 litres. Time = 120 \u00F7 4 = 30 minutes. \u2713

volume.question: An L-shaped solid is made by cutting a 5 cm \u00D7 3 cm \u00D7 4 cm corner from a 10 cm \u00D7 6 cm \u00D7 4 cm cuboid. What is the volume of the L-shaped solid?
volume.explanation: Full cuboid volume = 10 \u00D7 6 \u00D7 4 = 240 cm\u00B3. Cut-out corner = 5 \u00D7 3 \u00D7 4 = 60 cm\u00B3. L-shaped volume = 240 \u2212 60 = 180 cm\u00B3. \u2713

volume.question: All the water from a full rectangular tank measuring 15 cm \u00D7 10 cm \u00D7 8 cm is poured into an empty tank with a base of 20 cm \u00D7 12 cm. How deep is the water in the second tank?
volume.explanation: Volume of water = 15 \u00D7 10 \u00D7 8 = 1,200 cm\u00B3. Base area of second tank = 20 \u00D7 12 = 240 cm\u00B2. Depth of water = 1,200 \u00F7 240 = 5 cm. \u2713

volume.question: How many small cubes with 6 cm edges can be packed exactly into a box measuring 24 cm \u00D7 18 cm \u00D7 12 cm?
volume.explanation: Along the length: 24 \u00F7 6 = 4 cubes. Along the width: 18 \u00F7 6 = 3 cubes. Along the height: 12 \u00F7 6 = 2 cubes. Total = 4 \u00D7 3 \u00D7 2 = 24 cubes. \u2713
